target.title: fix(media): resolve absolute-path channel media for native vision blocks
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Telegram inbound images saved to disk are not passed to the model as native vision content blocks. The model receives only `<media:image>` placeholder text and cannot analyze the actual image. The same workflow works correctly through the webchat / Control UI.

`MediaAttachmentCache.resolveLocalPath()` skipped absolute-path validation — it returned `path.resolve(rawPath)` directly while relative paths went through `resolveUsableLocalCandidate`. Channel-delivered inbound media (Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.) use absolute filesystem paths from `saveMediaBuffer`. Separately, `resolveCurrentTurnImages()` discarded all images when any single read failed, so a single broken attachment could drop every image in the batch.

## Why This Change Was Made

The relative-path validation in `resolveLocalPath` (added in 610691c796) was the guardrail that should have also covered absolute paths — this extends it symmetrically. The all-or-nothing fallback in `resolveCurrentTurnImages` is replaced with a partial-success approach: resolved images are appended alongside extracted file images (PDF pages), and only a complete failure (zero resolved) falls back to the entries accumulated so far.

## User Impact

Telegram, Discord, Slack, and other channel users sending images to an OpenClaw bot with vision-capable models now get native vision analysis instead of silent `<media:image>` placeholder text. Normal paths (relative, webchat inline-image) are unaffected.

## Evidence

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/current-turn-images.test.ts
 ✓ hydrates Telegram-style state-relative media into native prompt images
 ✓ appends extracted PDF page images without dropping current image attachments
 ✓ orders extracted PDF page images before later current image attachments
 ✓ hydrates channel-delivered absolute-path media into native prompt images
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)
## Tests and validation
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/current-turn-images.test.ts — 4 passed (1 new + 2 upstream PDF + 1 existing relative-path)
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.media-paths.test.ts — 9 passed, including cross-platform path separator fix
What was not tested: Live Telegram/Discord bot end-to-end roundtrip.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: chenyangjun-xy
